prompt: Show me my Claude Code token usage and costs across all projects.
expected_output: Generates a complete report with global summary, plan ROI comparison, model breakdown, daily usage, and per-project breakdown for all time
assertions: invokes the bundled scripts/usage_report.py without date filters
includes both main session and subagent JSONL files
presents global summary with total tokens and API equivalent cost
shows plan ROI comparison for Pro, Max 5x, and Max 20x
shows per-model breakdown with input, output, cache read, and cache create columns
shows per-project breakdown sorted by cost descending
saves the report to ~/claude-code-usage-report.txt

prompt: How many tokens have I used since March 1st?
expected_output: Generates a report filtered to sessions from March 1st onward
assertions: passes --start-date 2026-03-01 to the bundled script
only includes messages with timestamps on or after the cutoff
shows daily usage starting from March 1st
does not include data from before the cutoff date

prompt: How much am I saving compared to API pricing?
expected_output: Shows API equivalent cost alongside all plan costs with savings percentages
assertions: invokes the bundled script which always includes plan ROI comparison
shows savings for Pro, Max 5x, and Max 20x plans
calculates period cost based on number of billing months
shows savings amount and percentage for each plan

prompt: Show me the token usage for this repository only.
expected_output: Generates a report scoped to the current project directory
assertions: identifies the current project from the working directory
passes --project with the project identifier to the bundled script
shows model breakdown and daily usage for that project only
does not include data from other projects

prompt: I was on pay-per-token until Feb 17, then switched to Max 5x. Show me the cost breakdown.
expected_output: Splits the report into two periods with separate cost calculations
assertions: runs the bundled script twice with different date ranges
first invocation uses --end-date for the pre-switch period
second invocation uses --start-date for the post-switch period
combines results and shows total actually paid vs total API equivalent

prompt: Generate a usage report for all my Claude Code sessions.
expected_output: Invokes the bundled script in a single Bash call
assertions: invokes scripts/usage_report.py in a single Bash call
does not generate a Python script from scratch
does not make separate tool calls per project or per file
resolves the script path relative to the skill installation directory

prompt: Update the pricing data for the usage report.
expected_output: Runs the script with --update-pricing to fetch latest prices and guide the user to update pricing.json
assertions: invokes scripts/usage_report.py with --update-pricing flag
shows current pricing.json values for comparison
directs the user to review the Anthropic pricing page
updates the checked date in pricing.json

prompt: How much does Claude Code cost?
expected_output: Asks the user to clarify the scope before generating a report
assertions: asks for date range or defaults to a reasonable period
asks whether to include all projects or current project only
does not guess or assume ambiguous parameters
